label: plain_text
text:
Note that in examples
s (a) to (e) a
above, there are only a finite
number of terms.
Such an AP is called a finite AP. Also note that each of these Arithmetic Progressions
(APs) has a last term. The APs in examples
s (i) to (v) i
in this section, are not finite APs
and so they
are called infinite Arithmetic Progressions. Such APs do not have a
last term.
